emergency.label: General Alt
emergency.number: 911 (also works, especially in the Holy Cities)

emergency.label: Pilgrim Unified Hotline
emergency.number: 1966 (Hajj/Umrah pilgrim support, Saudi SIM) or +966 92 000 2814 (international number)

entryRequirements.visaNote: IMPORTANT: entry to Mecca itself is restricted to Muslims only — this is a legal entry restriction, not a custom, and is strictly enforced at checkpoints on roads into the city. Non-Muslim travelers cannot visit Mecca at all. Muslim visitors need either a Hajj visa (Hajj season only, via the official Nusuk platform), an Umrah visa, or can perform Umrah on most other Saudi visa types including the eVisa — but must register on the Nusuk app.
entryRequirements.officialSource: Nusuk (official Saudi Hajj & Umrah platform) and Saudi eVisa portal (visa.visitsaudi.com)

transportInfo.paymentCard: Public transport is limited; most pilgrims move via organized group transport, taxis, or the Al Mashaaer Al Mugaddassah Metro (Hajj-season only, serves the holy sites)
transportInfo.note: Individual/independent tourist-style transport is far less relevant here than in other cities on this list — most visitor movement is organized through pilgrimage service providers.

publicHolidays2026: Saudi holidays are Hijri-calendar based (Eid al-Fitr, Eid al-Adha) plus Saudi National Day (Sep 23, fixed Gregorian date) and Founding Day (Feb 22, fixed).
majorEventsNote: This is a fundamentally different kind of entry in this dataset — Mecca is a pilgrimage destination with a hard religious entry restriction, not a general tourism city. The app should flag this distinctly rather than presenting it like a standard destination.
